Core Mission/Values.Stated: Vendelux event intelligence helps businesses meet more customers at in-person events. Transforming how companies discover, evaluate, and maximize the impact of events. Provides the system of record for event marketing, giving teams data and insights for smarter decisions.[1][3]
Core Mission/Values.Observed: Focus on data-driven event optimization for marketers, emphasizing pipeline and brand impact through AI and analytics.[3][6]

Module A.Problem Space: Event marketers drive pipeline and brand but events are one of the least optimized and most opaque marketing channels. Vendelux solves this with data and insights.[3]
Module A.Business Model: B2B SaaS platform for event intelligence and marketing optimization. Revenue from SaaS subscriptions. Growth stage: Series A or earlier startup environment. Financial health: 819 employees, hiring actively (25 open positions).[1][8]
Module A.Founding Story: No specific founding details or inflection points found in available sources.

Module B.The Big 3 Competitors: Direct rivals not explicitly identified in sources. Vendelux differentiates through event-specific intelligence, data/AI systems, and optimization for in-person events vs. opaque channels.[1][3]
Module B.Macro Trends: AI disruption in marketing tech; data privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA) impacting data-driven SaaS; rise of event marketing post-pandemic for pipeline generation.[1][6]

Module C.Financials/Funding: No recent funding rounds or earnings in 90-day window (since ~Feb 2026). Active hiring across 25 positions suggests growth.[8]
Module C.Organizational Shifts: No layoffs, hiring surges, or leadership changes reported in sources.
Module C.Product/Partnership News: No new launches or M&A in 90-day window. Product Manager role posted but removed Apr 22, 2026; Technical PM-Data role active (posted ~6 days ago).[1][6]

JD Analysis.Keywords: Product roadmap ownership, feedback synthesis, requirements definition, AI prototypes/wireframes (Figma, v0), engineering collaboration, UAT, metrics analysis, data privacy (GDPR/CCPA), B2B SaaS, startup (Series A/earlier).[1][2]
JD Analysis.Hidden Success Signals: Data-driven curiosity, simple UX delivery, stakeholder influence, tools (Figma, Linear/JIRA, v0), 5+ years PM with 2+ B2B SaaS, SQL/debugging for technical variant.[1][6]
JD Analysis.Required vs Preferred: Required: 5+ years PM (2+ B2B SaaS), startup experience, data metrics, privacy knowledge, comms skills, tools. Preferred: Technical depth (data modeling, ETL, AI, SQL) for Data PM variant.[1][6]

Module D.Hierarchy: Onsite NYC HQ role blending product/project/technical PM. Works with engineering, data/AI, analytics, GTM teams. No full org chart found.[1][6]

Module E.Operational Style: Hybrid/onsite NYC HQ required. Fast-paced early-stage startup environment.[1][6]

Module F.Priority Mapping: No specific Jason Taylor experience details provided. General bridges: Product roadmap/metrics align with Platform experience; data privacy/ETL/AI with Data Integrity; B2B SaaS delivery/feedback with Revenue Systems.[1][6]
